title: Determining prenatal, early childhood and cumulative long-term lead exposure using micro-spatial deciduous dentine levels.
ehr_evidence: The study uses biological samples (blood, bone, and teeth) from a prospective cohort, not EHR.
summary: Validation of micro-spatial dentine lead (Pb) levels as a biomarker for estimating prenatal, early childhood, and cumulative long-term lead exposure.
key_findings: Dentine Pb at birth significantly associated with cord blood Pb (r=0.69).
Prenatal dentine Pb associated with maternal patella and tibia Pb.
Dentine Pb at 3 months associated with childhood blood Pb (r=0.64).
Secondary dentine Pb correlated with cumulative blood lead index (r=0.38).
population: Children (3 months to 6 years) and mothers in a prospective pregnancy cohort
